titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 27

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=108 status=live modified=2017-01-30 19:27:59.847898+00 | translation moa=109 status=live modified=2016-06-07 10:59:52.971677+00

div.p:
Anno ab incarnatione domini . m° . cc° . xxii° . facta est hec conuentio inter dominum . S . priore sancti An
dRei ⁊ Conuentum eiusdem loci ex parte una . ⁊ Gillemor scoloc de Tarualont ho
minem eorum ligium ⁊ natiuum ex altera . videlicet quod idem .G. tanquam eorum natiuus ⁊ ligius
homo de eorum licentia erit cum domino . J . filio bone memorie . M . quondam comitis de Mar
quam diu ipsi priori ⁊ conuentui placuerit . ita quod idem . G . et eius liberi cum tota eorum sub
stantia sine alicuius contradictione uel aliquo impedimento cum dictis priori et conuentui placuerit
ad ipsos tanquam natiui homines sui reuertentur . quibus locum congruum ad habitandum bona fide
assignabunt . ⁊ si idem . G . uel eius liberi per annum uel amplius cum dicto domino . J . moram fecerint  an
nuatim soluent idem . G . uel eius liberi in recognitionem homagii sui memoratis priori et
conuentui . unam libram cere in assumptione beate Marie . ⁊ quia idem . G . sigillum proprium
non habuit conuencionem istam sigillo dicti domini . J . filii . M . comitis de Mar fecit sig
nari . ad maiorem etiam securitatem prefatus . G . tactis sacrosanctis iurauit se conuentionem
istam bona fide ⁊ sine contradictione aliqua fideliter seruaturum . hiis testibus . Domino . D . filio . M .
quondam comitis de
Mar . Domino . J . fratre eius . domino . P . de Maleuill' . uicecomite Abirdonensi . domino . R .
de Stradhehin . Beldin iudice de ferne . Willelmo capellano de Tarualont . Adam de Cus
senin . Maldouenin mac kelledered . killeserf de Rotheuen .

In the 1222nd year since the Incarnation of the Lord this agreement was made between the lord Simon, prior of St Andrews and the religious community of the same place on the one side, and on the other, Gillemuire the scoloc of Tarland, their liegeman and neyf: namely that the same Gillemuire, as their neyf and liegeman shall have permission to be with the lord James, son of Morgan of good memory, late earl of Mar, for as long as it pleases the prior and community, so that Gillemuire and his children, with all their belongings, without any objection or any impediment, should be returned to the said prior and convent as their neyfs when it shall pease the said prior and community; and they shall assign to them a suitable dwelling place in good faith. And if Gillemuire or his children should remain for a year or more with the said lord James, the same Gillemuire or his children shall pay one pound of wax annually on the Assumption of Blessed Mary in recognition of their homage to the said prior and community. And because the same Gillemuire does not have his own seal, he has caused this agreement to be sealed with the seal of the said lord James son of Morgan the earl of Mar. And for greater security, the aforementioned Gillemuire, touching holy relics, swore that he would faithfully uphold this agreement in good faith without any objection. With these witnesses: the lord Duncan, son of Morgan, late earl of Mar; the lord James, his brother; the lord Philip de Malville, sheriff of Aberdeen; the lord Robert of Strathearn; Beldin, judex of Fern; William, chaplain of Tarland; Adam of Cushnie; Mael Domnaig mac Gilla Doraid; Gilleserf of Ruthven.
